result:
Build finished successfully with `wpa_supplicant`, `wpa_cli`, and `wpa_passphrase` all linked. That warning in wpa_cli.c is pre-existing (not a build error) and outside the scope of the 5 compile errors, so left untouched.

All 5 compile errors fixed (root cause typos/mismatches, not workarounds):

1. `src/utils/eloop.c:615` — `timeout->time.seconds` → `timeout->time.sec` (struct field doesn't exist).
2. `wpa_supplicant/config_file.c:29` — `os_strlength(buf)` → `os_strlen(buf)` (undeclared function).
3. `src/rsn_supp/wpa.c:103` — `sm->pairwise_ciph` → `sm->pairwise_cipher` (no such struct member).
4. `wpa_supplicant/events.c:132` — `wpa_s->cur_ssid` → `wpa_s->current_ssid` (no such struct member).
5. `wpa_supplicant/scan.c:1137` — `eloop_register_timeout(sec, usec, wpa_supplicant_scan, wpa_s)` missing the trailing `NULL` timeout_ctx arg (function takes 5 params).

`make` in `wpa_supplicant/` now completes cleanly and produces `wpa_supplicant`, `wpa_cli`, `wpa_passphrase`.
